records: DIRECT REMATCH OF THE 2022 WORLD CUP SEMI-FINAL — MOROCCO SEEKING REVENGE: The last time these teams met was the 2022 World Cup semi-final in Qatar — France won 2-0 to end Morocco's historic run as the first African nation to reach the last four. France went on to lose the final to Argentina. Morocco are back at the same stage four years later with the explicit goal of rewriting that result.

MOROCCO HAVE NEVER BEATEN FRANCE IN SIX MEETINGS — EVER: France's all-time record against Morocco reads W4 D2 L0. Morocco have never beaten France in any competition in their entire football history. A win today would be not just Morocco's greatest result — it would be the first victory over France in six attempts spanning over 25 years.

FRANCE CHASING THREE CONSECUTIVE WORLD CUP SEMI-FINALS — ONLY GERMANY AND BRAZIL HAVE DONE IT: A win today sends France to a third straight World Cup semi-final. Only West Germany (1982-1990, four in a row) and Brazil (1994-2002, three in a row) have ever reached three consecutive World Cup semi-finals. France in 2026 would join that list. They lifted the trophy in 2018, reached the final in 2022, and are 90 minutes from the semi-final again.

DESCHAMPS — 25TH WC MATCH AS MANAGER, EQUALLING HELMUT SCHÖN'S ALL-TIME RECORD: Deschamps takes charge of France for the 25th time at a World Cup today — equalling West Germany's Helmut Schön (1966-1978) for the most matches managed at World Cup finals. A victory would break the record outright in the semi-final. He has already announced this is his final World Cup before stepping down.

MICHAEL OLISE — 5 ASSISTS, ONE FROM PELÉ'S SINGLE-TOURNAMENT RECORD: Olise has 5 assists in 6 World Cup matches — one behind Pelé's all-time record of 6 assists in a single tournament, set in 1958. He also became the first player since Brazil's Zico in 1978 to complete 10+ dribbles, 10+ chances created in open play, and 10+ through balls in their debut World Cup. A further assist today breaks a 68-year-old record held by the greatest player of his generation.

MBAPPÉ — 7 GOALS, JOINT-LEADING GOLDEN BOOT ALONGSIDE MESSI AND HAALAND: Mbappé is tied at 7 goals with Messi and Haaland — three players who have each defined this tournament. His tally of 19 all-time World Cup goals (before this match) sits just one behind Messi's all-time record of 20. A goal today pushes him to 20 and ties the greatest scorer in World Cup history.

ONLY TWICE HAS AN AFRICAN TEAM GONE UNBEATEN IN THEIR OPENING FIVE WC GAMES — MOROCCO BOTH TIMES: Morocco achieved this feat in 2022 and have done it again in 2026 — the only nation in history to go unbeaten across their first five matches at two separate World Cups. Their unbeaten tournament run extends to 10 matches dating back to Qatar.

MOROCCO'S 34-MATCH UNBEATEN RUN — LONGEST ACTIVE STREAK IN INTERNATIONAL FOOTBALL: Morocco have not lost an international match in 34 games, a run that includes the 2025 AFCON title, a perfect World Cup qualifying campaign, and now five matches at 2026. France's seven-match winning streak at this tournament meets the longest unbeaten run of any team in the world.

ACHRAF HAKIMI — 15 CHANCES CREATED, THE MOST BY ANY AFRICAN DEFENDER IN A SINGLE WC ON RECORD: Hakimi's 15 chances created at this tournament is the highest tally by an African defender in any single World Cup edition since records began in 1966. Across the last two World Cups, he has created 21 chances — more than any other defender worldwide across the same period.

ISMAEL SAIBARI OUT — MOROCCO LOSE THEIR JOINT-HIGHEST AFRICAN SINGLE-TOURNAMENT SCORER: Saibari's 4 goals at this World Cup equalled Roger Milla's African record for goals in a single tournament. He is now out with a hamstring injury — Morocco's most dangerous attacker is absent for the biggest match in their history. They scored 3-0 vs Canada without him, but the loss of their record-equalling scorer is enormous.

SIX MOROCCO PLAYERS WERE BORN IN FRANCE — INCLUDING BOUADDI, EL AYNAOUI AND HALHAL: Ayyoub Bouaddi, Neil El Aynaoui, Redouane Halhal, Samir El Mourabet, Issa Diop, and Gessime Yassine were all born in France and chose to represent Morocco. Several came through French youth academies. They now face the nation of their birth in a World Cup quarter-final — a dynamic that adds an extraordinary psychological dimension to an already loaded fixture.

SEVEN OF MOROCCO'S LAST NINE WORLD CUP DEFEATS CAME BY EXACTLY ONE GOAL: Morocco's margin of defeat at the World Cup is almost always razor-thin. A single goal — in either direction — is likely to decide this match. France's 14 goals in 6 games suggest they can provide it. Morocco's 34-match unbeaten run suggests they can prevent it.

SPAIN'S FIVE CONSECUTIVE WORLD CUP CLEAN SHEETS — A NATIONAL RECORD: Spain have kept clean sheets in every single match at this tournament — Saudi Arabia (4-0), Uruguay (1-0), Cape Verde (0-0 group draw), Austria (3-0), Portugal (1-0). Five consecutive WC clean sheets is the best defensive run in Spain's entire World Cup history. Belgium have scored 13 goals in 5 matches and are the most dangerous test of that record yet.

SPAIN FIRST WC SEMI-FINAL SINCE THEY WON IT IN 2010 — 16 YEARS: Spain's only World Cup semi-final since lifting the trophy in Johannesburg was in 2010 itself. They were eliminated in the Round of 16 in 2014, the group stage in 2018 (to Russia on penalties), and the Round of 16 in 2022 (to Morocco on penalties). A win today takes them to the semi-finals for the first time in 16 years.

BELGIUM HAVE NEVER BEATEN SPAIN IN ANY RECORDED MEETING: Spain's head-to-head record against Belgium reads W6 D1 L0 — Belgium have never won. Spain have scored 16 goals in those seven meetings, Belgium just three. Belgium's most recent competitive win over Spain came at the 1920 Olympics — 106 years ago. A win today would be the most historically improbable result in the 2026 knockout stage.

THE ONLY WORLD CUP MEETING: Spain beat Belgium 2-1 in the 1990 group stage — their only prior World Cup encounter. Belgium beat Spain on penalties in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final at the same stage as today — though that match is logged as a Spain win in most records. Both meetings came over 30 years ago. This is the most significant Spain vs Belgium match ever played.

LUKAKU — 90+ GOALS, 132 CAPS, FINAL WORLD CUP CHAPTER: Lukaku came off the bench to add a fourth against USA in the Round of 16, pushing him past 90 official international goals — Belgium's all-time record by a distance. At 33, playing in what is certainly his last World Cup, a goal today extends that record further into the unreachable.

LAMINE YAMAL — 18 YEARS OLD, FIRST WORLD CUP SEMI-FINAL WOULD COME THE DAY BEFORE HIS 19TH BIRTHDAY: Yamal turns 19 on July 13 — the day of the semi-finals. A win today means he would play a World Cup semi-final one day before his birthday. He is already the youngest scorer in European Championship history (Euro 2024). A goal or assist today in a World Cup quarter-final adds to one of the most remarkable teenage careers in football history.

CHARLES DE KETELAERE — ARRIVED WITH QUESTION MARKS, LEAVES WITH 3 GOALS IN 2 KNOCKOUT GAMES: The Atalanta midfielder had never scored for Belgium before this tournament. He now has three World Cup goals, including a brace against USA. No Belgium player has scored more times in knockout matches at a single World Cup. His emergence as a late-arriving box threat is the tactical problem Spain's backline has no scouting precedent for.

COURTOIS — EXTENDING HIS OWN ALL-TIME WC GOALKEEPER APPEARANCE RECORD: At 21 World Cup appearances entering this match, Courtois already holds the all-time record for a goalkeeper at the World Cup. Every minute he plays extends it further. He faces one of the tournament's most precise attacking units — and his record-breaking tournament continues regardless.

KEVIN DE BRUYNE — ZERO GOALS AND ZERO ASSISTS AT THIS WORLD CUP DESPITE LEADING BELGIUM IN CHANCES CREATED: De Bruyne has 0 goal contributions at this tournament despite creating the most chances of any Belgian player. At 35, this is his last World Cup. The greatest creative player of his generation has delivered nothing in his final attempt at the trophy. Spain's defensive structure — specifically Rodri — is built specifically to stop players like him.

SPAIN AND BELGIUM BOTH UNBEATEN — ONE OF THEM LOSES THEIR PERFECT RECORD TODAY: Spain are unbeaten in 36 consecutive matches including this tournament (W30 D6). Belgium are unbeaten in 18 consecutive competitive matches. One of these extraordinary unbeaten runs ends today. Spain's is the longer by a significant margin — but Belgium beat the co-hosts 4-1 just days ago.

MIKEL OYARZABAL — 4 GOALS IN 5 MATCHES, SPAIN'S TOURNAMENT TOP SCORER: Oyarzabal leads Spain's scoring with 4 goals — equalling his entire Spain career output before this tournament in just five matches. His movement in the penalty area and late arrivals have been impossible to defend. A fifth goal today would make him Spain's joint-highest scorer at a single World Cup since David Villa scored five in 2010.

HAALAND — 7 GOALS IN 4 GAMES, CHASING THE ALL-TIME SINGLE-TOURNAMENT RECORD: Haaland co-leads the Golden Boot with Messi on 8 and Mbappé on 7 goals. Just Fontaine's all-time record of 13 goals in one tournament (1958) is 6 away. Sándor Kocsis's record of 11 goals (1954) is 4 away. Gerd Müller's record among European players (10 in 1970) is 3 away. If Norway go deep, these records are genuinely in range.

HAALAND SCORED IN ALL 4 WORLD CUP MATCHES — IF HE SCORES TODAY HE JOINS AN EXCLUSIVE 5-GAME LIST: The last player to score in each of his first 5 games at a World Cup was Colombia's James Rodríguez in 2014. The last European to do so was Gerd Müller in 1970. Haaland scoring today joins a list that has not been updated for 12 years.

HAALAND HAS SCORED IN 14 CONSECUTIVE COMPETITIVE NORWAY MATCHES — 27 GOALS IN THAT STREAK: A national record for consecutive scoring matches. England's central defence — Guehi, Stones — faces the most in-form goalscorer on earth. A goal today extends it to 15.

HARRY KANE — 14 WORLD CUP GOALS, CLOSING ON THE TOP THREE ALL-TIME: Kane enters on 14 World Cup goals — two behind Ronaldo and Messi's tallies and four behind Miroslav Klose's all-time men's record of 16. He has scored in 11 of his last 12 knockout stage matches at major tournaments. A brace today takes him to 16 and ties Klose's record.

KANE HAS SCORED 11 GOALS IN HIS LAST 12 KNOCKOUT STAGE MATCHES AT MAJOR TOURNAMENTS: The most reliable big-game scorer in English football history. His last blank in a knockout match at a major tournament was Euro 2020. Norway's defensive record of 2.3 goals conceded per game at this tournament is the worst of any remaining quarter-finalist.

JUDE BELLINGHAM — FIRST MIDFIELDER TO SCORE 4+ GOALS IN A WORLD CUP FOR ENGLAND: Bellingham's brace against Mexico gave him 4 World Cup goals — the most ever by an England midfielder at a single tournament on Opta record since 1966. His 33.3% shot conversion rate (4 goals from 12 shots) is the highest of any England midfielder at a World Cup on record. He is 22 years old.

FIRST EVER WORLD CUP MEETING — AFTER 11 ALL-TIME ENCOUNTERS: England and Norway have met 11 times across friendlies and qualifying since 1937. England lead 6W-3D-2L. Yet this is the first time in history they have met at a World Cup finals. The most significant match in the history of this rivalry has no historical precedent at the tournament level.

NORWAY'S MOST FAMOUS WIN OVER ENGLAND — THE 1981 COMMENTATOR'S SPEECH: Norway's most iconic football moment is their 2-1 qualifier win in Oslo in 1981, when commentator Bjørge Lillelien's euphoric roll call of English icons ended with 'Maggie Thatcher, your boys took one hell of a beating.' Norway have beaten England only twice in 11 meetings. Today they attempt something far greater.

NORWAY BEAT BRAZIL — THE ONLY NATION BRAZIL HAVE NEVER BEATEN IN HISTORY: Brazil had never beaten Norway across four all-time meetings before this tournament. The 2-1 Round of 16 win extended Norway's unbeaten record against Brazil to five matches. They are the only nation to have played Brazil five or more times without losing. They now face England — a nation they have beaten twice in 11 attempts.

ENGLAND'S FIRST WC SEMI-FINAL SINCE 2018 ON THE LINE — JARELL QUANSAH SUSPENDED: England's 3-2 win over Mexico at the Azteca ended with Quansah's red card. He misses today, disrupting the defensive setup that has conceded in England's last three matches. England have reached the semi-finals only three times — 1966 (won), 1990 (lost), 2018 (lost). 60 years since their only trophy.

NORWAY'S FIRST WORLD CUP SEMI-FINAL WOULD BE THE GREATEST RESULT IN THEIR HISTORY — BY AN ENORMOUS MARGIN: Norway's previous World Cup best was the Round of 16 in 1998 — losing 1-0 to Italy. Their first-ever quarter-final is already history. A semi-final would be so far beyond anything Norway has achieved in football that no historical comparison within their own record exists. Haaland's father Alfie played in that 1998 tournament. Erling is now four years old at the time of his father's World Cup. He is now one win from a semi-final his father never came close to.

ØDEGAARD VS RICE — THE CENTRAL DUEL THAT DEFINES THE MATCH: Ødegaard has a direct goal involvement in every Norway knockout goal at this tournament. Rice leads the entire World Cup in ball recoveries per game and has the highest press success rate of any England midfielder. The battle between Norway's creator and England's destroyer in the middle of the park is the tactical equation both managers have spent the week solving.

MESSI — 8 GOALS, 21 ALL-TIME WORLD CUP GOALS, SCORED IN 9 CONSECUTIVE WORLD CUP MATCHES: Messi's winner against Egypt (his 8th of this tournament, 21st all-time) extended his consecutive WC scoring streak to 9 matches — a record with no historical parallel. He also scored a hat-trick in the group stage opener at this exact venue — Arrowhead Stadium. He returns to the scene of his first WC hat-trick at 2026 for a quarterfinal.

SWITZERLAND HAVE NEVER BEATEN ARGENTINA ACROSS ALL 7 HISTORICAL MEETINGS: Argentina's all-time record against Switzerland: W5 D2 L0. Switzerland have never scored more than one goal against Argentina in any meeting and have been kept scoreless four times. Their only non-defeat was a 1-1 draw in 2007. A win today would be the most historically improbable result of any remaining quarter-final.

MESSI — ONE GOAL FROM SURPASSING HIS OWN RECORD FOR GOALS IN A SINGLE WC EDITION: Messi has 8 goals at this tournament, equalling his own record from Qatar 2022. A 9th goal today breaks his own record for the most goals by any player in a single World Cup edition — a record he already holds. Two goals would give him 10 in one tournament — never done in the men's game.

ARGENTINA CAME BACK FROM 2-0 DOWN AGAINST EGYPT — THE GREATEST COMEBACK OF THIS WORLD CUP: Down 2-0 at the 67th minute, Argentina scored three goals in 25 minutes including Enzo Fernández's 92nd-minute winner. Messi missed a first-half penalty, then assisted the equaliser and scored Argentina's second goal himself. Coming from 2-0 down in a World Cup knockout match has only been done 7 times in history. Argentina add their name to that list.

SWITZERLAND'S FIRST WC QUARTERFINAL SINCE 1954 — AND THEY REACHED IT VIA PENALTY SHOOTOUT HEROICS: Gregor Kobel saved the decisive penalty vs Colombia to send Switzerland through 4-3 in a shootout after 120 goalless minutes. It is Switzerland's first World Cup quarterfinal in 72 years. The last time they reached this stage, they finished third (1954 host tournament). Today they attempt to go further than any Swiss side since.

SWITZERLAND ARE WITHOUT MANZAMBI, AEBISCHER AND JAQUEZ: Johan Manzambi — Switzerland's Player of the Tournament with 3 goals and 2 assists — is out with a knee injury. Michel Aebischer and Luca Jaquez are also unavailable. Three key players missing for the biggest match in Swiss football history in 72 years. Against Messi, this could be decisive.

ARGENTINA 11-MATCH UNBEATEN WC STREAK — APPROACHING THEIR OWN RECORD: Argentina have not lost a World Cup match in 11 games since the Saudi Arabia shock in 2022. Their all-time record unbeaten WC streak is 12 games (set between 1990-2006). A win today equals it. A further win in the semi-final breaks it.

MESSI PLAYED 31 WORLD CUP MATCHES — MORE THAN ANY ARGENTINIAN IN HISTORY: Messi has now appeared in 31 World Cup matches for Argentina — 10 more than Diego Maradona's 21. Every minute he plays at this tournament extends his own national record further into territory no Argentine will likely reach again.

MURAT YAKIN — MOST SUCCESSFUL SWISS WORLD CUP COACH IN HISTORY WITH A CHANCE TO GO FURTHER: Yakin already holds the record for most World Cup wins by a Swiss manager (5). A quarterfinal victory today would make him the first Swiss coach to reach a World Cup semi-final since Karl Rappan in 1954. He would also become the first coach from a non-South American, non-European nation to reach a WC semi... wait, he is Swiss/European. A semi-final would be Switzerland's deepest run in 72 years.

ARROWHEAD STADIUM — MESSI SCORED A HAT-TRICK HERE IN THE GROUP STAGE: Messi's tournament-opening hat-trick against Algeria at Arrowhead was one of the defining moments of the 2026 World Cup. He returns to the same ground for the quarterfinal. The stadium that witnessed the beginning of his record-breaking run now hosts what could be one of his final World Cup knockout matches.

ARGENTINA TRYING TO BECOME FIRST BACK-TO-BACK CHAMPIONS SINCE BRAZIL 1958-1962: A semi-final today keeps alive Argentina's bid to retain the World Cup — a feat only achieved twice in history: Italy (1934, 1938) and Brazil (1958, 1962). No nation has done it since Brazil 64 years ago. Messi, at 39, is the reason this attempt is credible.

GREGOR KOBEL VS EMILIANO MARTÍNEZ — TWO OF THE WORLD'S BEST GOALKEEPERS, BOTH PENALTY SHOOTOUT HEROES: Martínez saved a penalty in the 2022 World Cup final shootout against France, cementing Argentina's title. Kobel just saved the decisive penalty against Colombia to send Switzerland through. If this match goes to penalties, two of the greatest shootout goalkeepers at the 2026 tournament face each other.
